name: oraclaw-mcp-server
description: 17 decision intelligence algorithms as MCP tools for AI agents. Bandits (UCB1, Thompson, LinUCB), LP/MIP solver (HiGHS), Monte Carlo simulation, Bayesian inference, graph analytics (PageRank, Louvain), genetic algorithms, CMA-ES, anomaly detection, time series forecasting, ensemble models, and more. All under 25ms, deterministic, zero LLM cost.
author: Whatsonyourmind

tools.name: optimize_bandit
tools.description: Multi-armed bandit selection (UCB1, Thompson Sampling, epsilon-Greedy). Picks the best option given historical performance data.

tools.name: optimize_contextual
tools.description: Contextual bandit (LinUCB). Context-aware option selection using feature vectors.

tools.name: optimize_cmaes
tools.description: CMA-ES continuous optimization. Finds optimal parameter values for arbitrary objective functions.

tools.name: solve_constraints
tools.description: LP/MIP constraint solver (HiGHS). Finds optimal solutions to linear and mixed-integer programming problems.

tools.name: solve_schedule
tools.description: Task scheduling optimizer. Assigns tasks to time slots respecting deadlines, priorities, and energy constraints.

tools.name: analyze_graph
tools.description: Graph analytics — PageRank, Louvain community detection, shortest path. Analyzes network structure and importance.

tools.name: analyze_risk
tools.description: Portfolio VaR/CVaR risk analysis. Estimates financial risk using correlation matrices and Monte Carlo simulation.

tools.name: score_convergence
tools.description: Multi-source convergence scoring via Hellinger distance. Measures agreement across multiple probability estimates.

tools.name: predict_forecast
tools.description: Time series forecasting (Holt-Winters). Predicts future values from historical data with trend and seasonality.

tools.name: detect_anomaly
tools.description: Anomaly detection (Z-score, IQR). Flags outliers in data using statistical methods.

tools.name: plan_pathfind
tools.description: A* pathfinding with K-shortest paths (Yen's algorithm). Finds optimal and alternative paths through graphs.

tools.name: simulate_montecarlo
tools.description: Monte Carlo simulation with 6 distribution types. Estimates outcome probabilities through random sampling.
